title: Define Uniface Server Types (USTs)
text: Define Uniface Server Types (USTs) Each UST identifies a specific Uniface Server configuration. It can be defined in the assignment file of the Uniface Router, or it can be provided to the Uniface Router by a Uniface Server that has been started manually. Create an assignment file for each Uniface Server to contain default paths for locating databases, files, and components. For example, for a Uniface Server that has the UST (Uniface Server name) Sales , you could create sales.asn : Explain this code ;sales.asn [PATHS] $DATA = MSS:DB:MAIN|user|pass [ENTITIES] *.* = $DATA:*.* Copy Define the UST in the [SERVERS] section of the Uniface Router's assignment file. For example: Explain this code [SERVERS] Sales= userver.exe /asn=sales.asn /maxidle=120 HR=userver.exe /asn=hr.asn Copy If you start a Uniface Server manually (instead of having the Uniface Router do it), specify the UST on the command line using the /ust switch. For example: Explain this code userver.exe /asn=sales.asn /dnp=TCP:ahost+13002 /ust=Sales Copy Related Links urouter.asn Assignment file for the Uniface Router. You can use the urouter.asn file to specify settings, paths to connectors, and Uniface Server environment definitions, such as USTs. /ust Register a client application or Uniface Server under a symbolic name with a Uniface Router. Configuring the Uniface Server You need to configure the Uniface Server so that it can find the databases and Uniface files, entities, and components that it needs. Was this topic helpful?
